title: Navigating the New Dawn: Unpacking the Income Tax Department's Latest FAQs on the Income Tax Act, 2025
content:
# Navigating the New Dawn: Unpacking the Income Tax Department's Latest FAQs on the Income Tax Act, 2025

As a tax professional in India, the phrase "new tax regime" often brings a mix of anticipation and a touch of apprehension, doesn't it? We've all been keenly watching the horizon since the **Income Tax Act, 2025** came into force on **April 1, 2026**. The transition from the venerable Income Tax Act, 1961, to this new framework is a monumental shift, and naturally, it has brought with it a cascade of questions from taxpayers and practitioners alike. The Income Tax Department, acknowledging these complexities, has once again stepped up, releasing updated FAQs on the transition, comprehensively updated as of **July 18, 2026**. This isn't just a routine update; it's a crucial guide to help us all sail smoothly through this sea of change.

### The Big Picture: Why These FAQs Matter Now More Than Ever

The move to the **Income Tax Act, 2025**, accompanied by the **Income-tax Rules, 2026**, signifies a complete overhaul of our direct tax landscape. With new provisions, amended sections, and revised compliance procedures, the initial phase of implementation has certainly presented its share of learning curves. These updated FAQs aim to clear the air, addressing practical challenges and ambiguities that have surfaced. They act as a critical bridge, ensuring that the spirit of the new law is understood and implemented uniformly across the board, reducing potential litigation and compliance burdens.

The official portal itself confirms that the "Income-tax Act, 2025 comes into force from 1st April, 2026", with "Income-tax Rules, 2026" also in effect. The FAQs clarify how these new provisions are to be interpreted, particularly in situations where there's a continuity or discontinuity with the erstwhile Act.

### Key Highlights and Deep Dive into the Transition Mechanisms

Let's dissect some of the critical areas clarified by the Income Tax Department:

#### 1. Effective Date and Applicability: No More Guesswork
The FAQs firmly reiterate that the **Income Tax Act, 2025**, is effective from **April 1, 2026**, applicable for the Assessment Year 2026-27 onwards. This means all transactions, incomes, and expenses pertaining to the Financial Year 2025-26 will be governed by the new Act. The clarifications address specific scenarios where a transaction might straddle both the old and new regimes, providing guidance on how to determine the applicable provisions. For instance, any income accrued or arising on or after April 1, 2026, will fall squarely under the new Act.

#### 2. Transitioning Carry Forward of Losses and Unabsorbed Depreciation
One of the most pressing concerns for businesses was how existing accumulated losses and unabsorbed depreciation, computed under the **Income-tax Act, 1961**, would be treated under the new regime. The FAQs provide much-needed clarity. It's good news, really; the ability to carry forward and set off losses (including business losses, capital losses, and unabsorbed depreciation) from prior assessment years is largely preserved, albeit with specific adjustments and conditions laid out in the new Act. Taxpayers need to carefully review the new rules on the quantum and period for which such carry-forwards are permitted, ensuring they align their record-keeping accordingly.

#### 3. New Forms and Compliance Procedures: Get Ready!
The Department has confirmed that the Income Tax Return forms – **ITR-1, ITR-2, ITR-3, and ITR-4 for Assessment Year 2026-27 are now live** on the e-filing portal. This means the necessary utilities (Excel Utilities, Online, and Offline filing options) are enabled, providing taxpayers ample time to prepare. The FAQs delineate the changes in reporting requirements within these new forms. For instance, certain schedules might have been rationalized, or new disclosures mandated, reflecting the policy shifts under the **Income Tax Act, 2025**. It’s high time to explore these new forms and understand the revised data points required.

#### 4. Relief and Exemptions under the New Act
The FAQs also shed light on specific exemptions and reliefs. For example, recent notifications from the CBDT, such as Notification No. 80/2026, have clarified that certain payments like interest, dividends, professional fees, commission, and brokerage received by eligible IFSC Units will be exempt from TDS under the **Income-tax Act, 2025**. Similarly, Notification No. 75/2026 and 74/2026 specifically addressed non-deduction of TDS on ship and aircraft lease rent paid to IFSC Units under Section 147 of the new Act. Such specific clarifications are a big relief for industries operating in these specialized sectors.

#### 5. Comparison Tool: Your Go-To Resource
Did you know the Income Tax Department's official portal offers an **"IT Bill Comparison Tool"**? This fantastic feature allows you to "Compare Income-tax Act 1961 with Income-tax Act 2025". While not explicitly part of the FAQs, its mention reinforces the Department's push for clarity during this transition. I strongly recommend using this tool to understand section-by-section changes, which can be invaluable when interpreting the new FAQs.

### Understanding the Old vs. New: A Quick Glance

While the full breadth of changes is extensive, here's a simplified comparative table highlighting hypothetical, yet commonly impacted, areas during a tax regime shift. Please remember, for specific provisions, referring to the official Act and Rules is paramount.

| Feature / Provision         | Old Provision (Under ITA, 1961)         | New Provision (Under ITA, 2025)             | Implications                                   |
| :-------------------------- | :-------------------------------------- | :------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------- |
| **Tax Slabs (Illustrative)**| More complex, higher slabs for certain incomes | Rationalized, potentially fewer slabs, simplified criteria | Aims for broader compliance and ease of understanding |
| **Depreciation Rates**      | Schedule-based, specific rates for assets | Revised schedules, potentially updated rates or block definitions | May impact financial statements and tax liability for businesses |
| **Certain Deductions**      | Many specific deductions available       | Rationalized; some deductions removed, others streamlined | Requires re-evaluation of tax planning strategies |
| **Compliance Timelines**    | Standardized dates                      | Potentially revised dates for certain filings | Critical to check the new tax calendar |

### The Way Forward: What Professionals and Taxpayers Should Do

The updated FAQs are more than just a document; they are a clear indication from the Income Tax Department of its commitment to facilitate a smooth transition. For us, as tax professionals, the task is clear:

*   **Deep Dive into the FAQs:** Go through each question and answer meticulously. Don't just skim; internalize the nuances, especially regarding transitional provisions.
*   **Leverage the Comparison Tool:** Actively use the "IT Bill Comparison Tool" on the official website to understand the legislative changes backing these FAQs.
*   **Educate and Advise:** It's our responsibility to keep our clients and colleagues informed. Conduct internal training sessions, write client advisories, and ensure everyone is aware of the revised compliance landscape.
*   **Stay Updated:** Tax laws are dynamic. Even with these FAQs, future clarifications or amendments may come. Regularly check the Income Tax Department's portal for new notifications and circulars.
*   **Review Financial Systems:** Ensure that accounting and tax software are updated to reflect the new provisions of the **Income Tax Act, 2025**, and the **Income-tax Rules, 2026**.

This transition, while challenging, also presents an opportunity to streamline our practices and adopt a more forward-looking approach to taxation. By diligently understanding these FAQs, we can confidently guide our stakeholders through the new regime. *Tension lene ki zaroorat nahi hai, bas thodi padhai aur vigilance chahiye!*

## Read the Official Document
* [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.incometax.gov.in/)

*Published on: 19 July 2026*
